Case Study

From a blank slate to a fully simulated global business challenge

An eight-phase business simulation with live gamification that transformed a disconnected management development programme into a rigorous, decision-driven leadership experience for mid-to-senior commercial managers.

Client
Leading Consumer Goods Organisation
Sector
Consumer Goods / FMCG
Timeline
Five months
At a glance
8
Simulation phases — each a real leadership decision point
97%
Post-programme readiness confidence across all participants
0
Source content at project start — entire curriculum built from scratch
1
Coherent simulation — modular SCORM architecture, phase by phase
The challenge

Mid-level managers making senior decisions without the muscle memory to do it.

Leading Consumer Goods Organisation was accelerating its regional expansion into Southeast Asia. Its talent pipeline had a critical gap: commercially capable mid-managers who had never operated at business unit level — who had never owned a P&L, negotiated with a sceptical CFO, or designed a route-to-market under regulatory pressure.

Classroom training had been tried. It produced knowledge, not capability. The organisation needed its managers to experience the complexity of senior decision-making — not read about it.

  • No usable source content existed — only a high-level competency framework and a set of aspirational leadership behaviours
  • The simulation had to function simultaneously as an assessment tool and a development experience, with performance data meaningful to HR leadership
  • The business context had to be realistic enough to be credible, but fictionalised to avoid competitive sensitivity concerns
  • A geographically dispersed development team operated across three time zones, requiring disciplined asynchronous sprint collaboration
  • The programme had to serve both first-time cohorts and re-deployment groups seeking an advanced-level challenge
The approach

An eight-phase business simulation — each phase a real leadership crucible, with gamification engineered to sustain intensity and competitive energy across 32 hours of programme runtime.

01
Market Entry Analysis
Teams analyse a $4.2B market opportunity, assess competitive dynamics, and make an entry recommendation against a 40-minute clock.
Strategic Thinking
02
Competitive Intelligence
Deep-dive into two dominant incumbents using curated market intelligence. Teams must identify exploitable gaps and build a differentiation thesis.
Analytical Rigour
03
Financial Modelling
Build a three-scenario P&L, stress-test assumptions live, then defend the financial model in a Board Challenge with a sceptical CFO.
Financial Acumen
04
Stakeholder Negotiation
Reach alignment with four stakeholders — each with conflicting agendas and specific levers that must be discovered and deployed in real time.
Influence & Persuasion
05
Operations Design
Design the supply chain, distribution architecture, and operating model for the new business unit within fixed cost and lead-time constraints.
Operational Thinking
06
Risk & Compliance
Navigate three live regulatory scenarios — including an unexpected policy change mid-phase — while keeping the launch timeline intact.
Risk Management
07
Go-to-Market Launch
Execute market entry. Allocate budget across channels, respond to a competitive counter-move, and hit Q1 milestones under Board scrutiny.
Commercial Execution
08
Performance Review
Teams present full business unit performance to the Board, connecting every prior decision to the final P&L outcome. Nothing is forgotten.
Accountability & Reflection
↑
Decisions compound — consequences cascade
Unlike episodic simulations that reset between modules, the simulation remembers. An aggressive Phase 01 entry creates tighter margins in Phase 03 and a harder CFO challenge in Phase 07. Every decision lives on.
Live team leaderboard
Four competing teams accumulate points across all eight phases. Rankings update in real time, creating genuine competitive tension that sharpens every decision.
Achievement badge system
Six unlockable badges reward behaviours beyond correct answers — speed, collaboration, precision, and consistency — ensuring engagement at every level of capability.
Summative assessment gates
Each phase locks until the assessment is cleared. Progression is earned, not assumed — creating the psychological weight of real-world accountability at every stage.
The design insight
The problem we solved

Building a complete curriculum from a competency framework alone

The client had no training content. No case library. No documented processes. Every scenario, decision node, and stakeholder persona had to be constructed through intensive SME extraction sessions — six workshops across three weeks — before a single screen was built.

The turning point A working Phase 01 prototype was built in week two — before full content was agreed — to give the client something to react to rather than approve in the abstract. The response was immediate: it provoked the richest content conversations of the entire project and accelerated the SME process significantly.
What made this work

Simulation designed around real leadership decisions, not theory

Rather than teaching commercial leadership as abstract knowledge, the programme placed participants inside actual business decisions. Each of the eight phases recreated the judgements, trade-offs, and pressures a senior leader would face — from financial modelling under board scrutiny to negotiating with a CFO whose approval could not be assumed.

The full simulation arc was presented at the outset and revisited at each phase gate, reinforcing the mental model of the complete leadership challenge even as participants worked through individual stages in depth.

Programme in action
01
Programme overview & eight-phase simulation map
02
Phase entry — gamification reward dashboard
03
Market entry analysis — team decision screen
04
Financial modelling — P&L build & Board challenge
05
Stakeholder negotiation scenario
06
Summative assessment & phase completion screen
The outcome

Managers entered their next role faster, sharper, and with decisions already banked.

The organisation was able to reduce the average time-to-promotion for high-potential managers by 14 months, directly attributing the accelerated readiness to the programme. The simulation replaced a 12-month stretch assignment process that had previously been the only credible preparation route.

360-degree feedback gathered six months post-programme showed a statistically significant improvement in financial confidence, stakeholder communication, and strategic framing — the three competency areas the simulation was designed to develop.

"For the first time, we had a development programme that actually changed behaviour — not just knowledge scores. Our managers came out of the simulation ready to make the kinds of calls we'd previously only trusted directors to make."

— Chief People Officer, Leading Consumer Goods Organisation

97%
Post-programme confidence All participants expressed readiness for increased responsibility at programme completion
8
End-to-end leadership coverage Every critical commercial leadership challenge simulated within a single coherent programme
↑41%
Internal promotion rate Increase in promotions among programme participants within 12 months of completion
100%
Facilitator credibility rating All facilitators rated the simulation content as highly realistic and commercially credible
